Dynamic programming is a way to solve problems by breaking them into smaller parts that can be solved once and reused. It works best when the problem has *overlapping sub‑problems* – the same small problem appears many t

BFS (Breadth‑First Search) • Visits all nodes level by level, starting from the chosen root. • Uses a queue to keep track of the next node to explore. • Finds the shortest path in an unweighted graph. • Needs more memory
